metadata.title:
FIRST WHITE Child


BORN IN SPOKANE

pages.raw_text:
of the old soldier barracks, which
stood in the middle of what is now
Sprague avenue.


The ladies who came to see me and
said l was the first white child born
there were Mrs. ]. J. Browne, Mrs.
A. M. Cannon. Mrs. ]. N. Grover, Mrs.
w. c. Gray, whose husband kept the
first hotel there, and Mrs. Warner.


In the fall of 1879 my father and
mother crossed the river in little
boat, as there was no bridge, to there
homestead on Five Mile prairie.
There | was raised until 1895, when
we sold out and went to California.
| would like to know if you could
find out by records if | was the first
white child born in Spokane, for 1
do not wish to say l am the first If
/ am not


would like to know if Spokane
would acknowledge me as one of it's
pioneers Hoping to hear from you
soon | am


Very Respectfully.
Mrs. Lille II. Wilbur,
Portland, Ore.
